retrieval_text: Mullein is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 77 works and 155 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Mullein. Common plant-part co-mentions include Leaf, Root, Flower, Bark, Seed, Gum. Common preparation co-mentions include Oil, Decoction, Tea, Ointment, Poultice, Wash. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Inflammation, Cough, Fever, Wounds or ulcers, Constipation. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Child-use caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Bleeding caution, Fatal-risk warning. Representative source works include Dr. Mansperger's book of hygiene : containing instructions for the cure of diseases, together with many valuable and useful prescriptions : added to which is an accurate description of the properties and application of the curative powers of roots and herbs, with instructions for a diagnosis of diseases from the urine and pulse; Herbal simples : approved for modern uses of cure / by W. T. Fernie.; Popular medicine, or, The American family physician : a work in which the principles and practice of medicine are familiarly explained : comprising a complete and comprehensive description of diseases, with full and definite directions for their treatment : the properties of many new and important medicines, with their doses and mode of administration, are accurately described : designed for general use and adapted to all climates; Herbal Simples Approved for Modern Uses of Cure. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
